answers.goals: Keep extending.md's engine/content boundary structural: core (checks/, migrations/, mount/, routines/, .github/, the top-level docs, and the shared engine modules under packs/ and skills/) must never name a specific pack or skill — packs and skills are discovered content, and a core file citing one couples the engine to its content. One rule per namespace (packs/*, skills/*); reviewed inherent couplings live in each rule's except.

rules.reason: core is the engine that runs pack content — it must not name the specific packs it runs (extending.md)

except.reason: core CI invokes the promote write-surface gate that #279 relocated into the canon-curation pack (node packs/canon-curation/promote-scope.mjs) — a structural cross-reference like the pack-test globs, pending CI discovering it structurally
decouple(release-infra): the core .github/ README documents the chrome-extension-release plumbing being vendored out of core (#276)
decouple(release-infra): chrome-extension-release reusable release infra hosted in the core .github/ tree; being vendored out (#276)
decouple(release-infra): chrome-extension-release reusable release infra hosted in the core .github/ tree; being vendored out (#276)
decouple(release-infra): chrome-extension-release reusable release infra hosted in the core .github/ tree; being vendored out (#276)
decouple(release-infra): a chrome-release composite action in the core .github/ tree; being vendored out (#276)
inherent: a one-time seed migration necessarily names the single pack it seeds; it self-retires once the fleet converges
inherent: a one-time seed migration necessarily names the single pack it seeds; it self-retires once the fleet converges
inherent: a one-time vendoring migration necessarily names the pack whose plumbing it relocates into consumers; it self-retires once the fleet converges

inherent(operational seed): the one-time seed one-liner (bootstrap step 4b) backfills these default-on packs into an existing declaration while their self-retiring seed migrations are live; retires with those migrations

rules.reason: core is the engine that surfaces skill content — it must not name the specific skills it runs (extending.md)

except.reason: decouple(release-infra): a chrome-release composite action in the core .github/ tree cites the git/GitHub skill; being vendored out (#276)

accept.reason: pack check modules deliberately share the checks/ engine lib — a named cross-cutting concern per skills/file-placement/SKILL.md; packs are organized by pack, not by dependency

a skill's check-the-work modules deliberately share the checks/ engine lib (findings.mjs) — the same named cross-cutting concern as the packs/ acceptance; checks are co-located with the skill whose action they validate, not organized by dependency

the core planner deliberately imports the pack-discovery registry (packs/registry.mjs — core machinery per extending.md, not a specific pack) to enumerate each repo's run_daily tasks; discovery lives under packs/ and the planner under routines/, so the cross-tree reach is inherent and correct, not a misplacement

the engine's file-set builder deliberately imports the consumer-layout constant SHARED_SUBDIR (beside LOCAL_PACKS_SUBDIR in packs/registry.mjs — core machinery per extending.md, not a specific pack) so the shared-mount sweep exclusion and the vendor writer share one definition; the cross-tree reach is inherent, not a misplacement
